rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2019 in Department 503, Line 1. Wilden Pump Company's motion for summary judgment is off calendar per the written request of the moving party. = (503/CML)

On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2019 in Department 503, Line 1. Blackmer Pump Company's motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: GLORIA LUZ MARTINEZ ET AL VS. ASBESTOS COMPANIES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Dismiss Complaint For Failure To Effectuate Service Within Three Years Of Filing Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2019 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Daimler Trucks North America, LLC f/k/a Freightliner's LLC's motion to dismiss complaint for failure to effectuate service within three years of the filing of the complaint pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. § 583.210 is off calendar. Dismissal filed as to Defendant Daimler Trucks North America, LLC f/k/a Freightliner LLC on February 27, 2019. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA GLAVIN ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC.

Defendant Pace, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or in the alternative summary adjudication is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
